meta.note: Temporal supersession set (18 cases). Still modest — read the CI widths, not the point estimates. Construction: LongMemEval-style fact decomposition (arXiv:2410.10813). Queries are paraphrases (shared vocabulary with the notes, but not verbatim slices of either body) to limit train/test leakage; the harness prints a leakage check. Each superseded note is deliberately MORE term-dense on the query terms than its current counterpart, so the OFF arm favours the stale note — isolating the supersession demotion as the only lever that can flip the result. Diverse domains (tools, policies, formats, conventions) so the result isn't an artefact of one phrasing.

current.title: pgvector for embeddings
current.body: We keep vector embeddings in Postgres using the pgvector extension.

superseded.title: Pinecone for embeddings
superseded.body: Our vector embeddings store is Pinecone. Pinecone holds the vector embeddings; the embeddings vector store is the Pinecone service for vector embeddings.

cases.query: how frequently are API tokens refreshed

current.title: Rotate tokens daily at the gateway
current.body: API tokens rotate automatically every 24 hours at the gateway.

superseded.title: Manual weekly token rotation
superseded.body: We rotate API tokens manually. Token rotation is weekly; rotate tokens by hand. Token rotation cadence for API tokens is the weekly manual token rotation.

cases.query: in which sequence do order events fire

current.title: OrderPriced fires before OrderConfirmed
current.body: Order events emit OrderPriced before OrderConfirmed so confirmation carries the final price.

superseded.title: OrderConfirmed before OrderPriced
superseded.body: Order lifecycle events: OrderConfirmed before OrderPriced. The order events ordering puts order confirmation first, then order pricing in the order event sequence.

current.title: Local SQLite FTS5 index
current.body: The search index is a local SQLite FTS5 database.

superseded.title: Elasticsearch for search
superseded.body: The search index runs on Elasticsearch. Elasticsearch backs the search; the search index cluster is Elasticsearch serving the search index for full-text search index queries.

superseded.title: Deploy to Heroku
superseded.body: We deploy the service to Heroku. The service deploy target is Heroku; deploys run on Heroku dynos and the service deploy pipeline pushes the service to Heroku.

cases.query: what do we use for caching

current.title: In-process LRU cache
current.body: Caching is an in-process LRU cache.

superseded.title: Redis cache cluster
superseded.body: We use Redis for caching. The cache layer is a Redis cache cluster; caching uses Redis, the Redis cache serves all caching for the cache layer.

cases.query: what message queue do we run

current.title: NATS for messaging
current.body: Our message queue for messaging is NATS.

superseded.title: RabbitMQ message queue
superseded.body: The message queue is RabbitMQ. RabbitMQ is our messaging broker; the RabbitMQ message queue handles messaging, and the message queue broker is RabbitMQ.

current.title: OAuth2 via the identity provider
current.body: Users authenticate with OAuth2 through the identity provider.

superseded.title: Cookie session authentication
superseded.body: Users authenticate with session cookies. Cookie-based authentication: the login sets a session cookie; authentication relies on the session cookie for user authentication.

cases.query: what API style do services expose

current.title: gRPC service APIs
current.body: Services expose gRPC APIs as the API style.

superseded.title: REST APIs
superseded.body: Services expose REST APIs. The API style is REST; REST endpoints expose the service API, and the REST API style defines the service API surface.

current.title: GitHub Actions for CI
current.body: Continuous integration runs on GitHub Actions.

superseded.title: Jenkins CI server
superseded.body: Continuous integration runs on Jenkins. The CI server is Jenkins; Jenkins pipelines run CI, and the Jenkins CI runner executes the continuous integration jobs.

current.title: UUIDv7 identifiers
current.body: Entity identifiers use the UUIDv7 format.

superseded.title: Auto-increment integer ids
superseded.body: Entity identifiers are auto-increment integers. The identifier format is a sequential integer; integer primary keys give each entity an integer identifier in integer format.

current.title: Trunk-based development
current.body: Our git branching model is trunk-based development.

superseded.title: GitFlow branching
superseded.body: Our git branching model is GitFlow. GitFlow uses release branches and feature branches; the GitFlow branching model defines our branching with long-lived branches.

current.title: Structured JSON logs
current.body: Application logs are structured JSON logs.

superseded.title: Plaintext line logs
superseded.body: Application logs are plaintext line logs. Logging writes unstructured plain text log lines; the log format is plain text and logs are text log lines.

current.title: Secrets in cloud KMS
current.body: Application secrets are stored in the cloud KMS.

superseded.title: Secrets in dotenv files
superseded.body: Application secrets are stored in dotenv files. Secrets live in .env environment files; the secret store is dotenv, and secrets are kept in env files.

cases.query: how do we retry failed calls

current.title: Exponential backoff with jitter
current.body: Failed calls retry with exponential backoff and jitter.

superseded.title: Fixed-interval retries
superseded.body: Failed calls retry on a fixed interval. The retry policy is a constant retry interval; retries use fixed-delay retries and a fixed retry interval for every retry.

cases.query: how are service messages serialized on the wire

current.title: Protobuf wire format
current.body: The wire format for service messages is Protobuf.

superseded.title: JSON over the wire
superseded.body: The wire format for service messages is JSON. JSON payloads go over the wire; the JSON wire format serializes service messages as JSON on the wire.

cases.query: which framework do we run tests with

current.title: pytest test suite
current.body: The test framework running our suite is pytest.

superseded.title: unittest framework
superseded.body: The test framework running our suite is unittest. unittest TestCase classes are the test framework; the unittest framework runs the tests in the test suite.

current.title: Config-file feature flags
current.body: We gate features with config-file feature flags.

superseded.title: LaunchDarkly feature flags
superseded.body: We gate features with LaunchDarkly. LaunchDarkly is our feature flag service; feature flagging and feature gating use LaunchDarkly remote feature flags.

current.title: Token-bucket rate limiting
current.body: Request rate limiting is enforced with a token-bucket limiter.

superseded.title: Fixed-window rate limiting
superseded.body: Request rate limiting is enforced with a fixed window. The rate limiting uses a fixed-window counter; fixed-window rate limiting counts requests per window for rate limiting.

distractors.title: Code style
distractors.body: We use four-space indentation and type hints across the codebase.

distractors.title: Standup
distractors.body: The daily standup happens at ten in the morning in the team channel.

distractors.title: Release cadence
distractors.body: A release is cut every second Thursday afternoon.

distractors.title: Onboarding
distractors.body: New engineers pair with a buddy for their first two weeks.

distractors.title: Docs site
distractors.body: The documentation site is published from the docs directory on each merge.
